title: Trade-off Relation for Black Hole Entropy Fluctuations
abstract: Black holes respond to infalling quantum matter fields by changing their entropy. Since such matter is quantum in nature, the entropy response should be sensitive to its quantum fluctuations. We show, within stochastic semiclassical gravity, that a horizon cannot record relevant quantum information with arbitrarily small entropy fluctuations. For the infalling photons encoding which-path information in the Danielson-Satishchandran-Wald decoherence experiment, we derive a trade-off relation between the stochastic variance of the black hole entropy change and the photon number.
summary: This paper establishes a fundamental limit on the precision with which a black hole can record quantum information, showing that entropy fluctuations are unavoidable. It proves that the relative variance of the entropy change is inversely proportional to the number of infalling photons. This result links the stochastic nature of semiclassical gravity to the information-carrying capacity of quantum fields.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether black hole horizons can record quantum information with arbitrarily small entropy fluctuations and seeks to establish a fundamental trade-off relation between entropy variance and the amount of infalling quantum information.
main_result: The authors derive a fundamental trade-off relation where the relative variance of the black hole entropy change is bounded from below by the inverse of the photon number: Var[Delta delta S] / (E[Delta delta S])^2 >= 1 / (4 <N>).
method: The study utilizes stochastic semiclassical gravity and the Einstein-Langevin equations, employing the Magnus expansion, the Cauchy-Schwarz inequality, and the gravitational phase space method to analyze fluctuations in the energy-momentum tensor.
model_or_system: The system consists of an asymptotically flat, stationary Schwarzschild black hole interacting with infalling quantum matter fields (photons) within the framework of the Danielson-Satishchandran-Wald decoherence mechanism.
key_observables: Stochastic variance of black hole entropy change (Var[Delta delta S]), expectation value of entropy change (E[Delta delta S]), photon number (<N>), and quantum mutual information (I).
important_parameters: Planck length (l_P), Schwarzschild radius (r_h), effective dipole moment (ql), and the number of entangling photons.
main_assumptions: The field state is assumed to be a quasifree Hadamard state, and the spacetime is assumed to be stationary and asymptotically flat.
figures_summary: Figure 1 provides a Penrose diagram of a black hole and a schematic of the DSW experiment, illustrating a superposed charge and the capture of entangling photons by the horizon.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the physical problem and the DSW decoherence mechanism, proceeds to establish the mathematical framework of stochastic semiclassical gravity, derives the fundamental trade-off relations and uncertainty bounds using field-theoretic methods, and concludes with implications for horizon area fluctuations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The Penrose diagram of a black hole with stationary trajectories of the superposed charge and a schematic picture of the DSW experiment. No radiation will be propagated to I + so long as the experiment is performed adiabatically. Here, V1 and V2 are two “times” on the horizon H, and C is a cross section of the black hole.
